qa_pairs.question: What is the Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Canadian Common CV (CCV) is a software system funded by the Canadian Institutes of Health Research (CIHR) aimed at standardizing CV submissions to granting agencies for funding competitions.

qa_pairs.question: Why does the Alliance require faculty users to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance requires faculty users to submit a CCV for renewing their Alliance account and for Principal Investigators (PIs) and co-Principal Investigators (co-PIs) when applying for Resource Allocation Competitions (RAC).

qa_pairs.question: Which major funding agencies, in addition to the Alliance, currently use the Canadian Common CV (CCV) system?
qa_pairs.answer: Major funding agencies that currently use the Canadian Common CV (CCV) system include NSERC, SSHRC, NCE, and CIHR.

qa_pairs.question: Who is generally required to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV) for Alliance account renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: All users with Faculty and Adjunct Faculty roles are generally required to submit a Canadian Common CV (CCV) for Alliance account renewal.

qa_pairs.question: Are there any exceptions for faculty users regarding mandatory Canadian Common CV (CCV) submission for Alliance account renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, submitting a Canadian Common CV (CCV) is optional for Emeritus Professors, Librarians, and Retired Faculty when renewing their Alliance account.

qa_pairs.question: Who is required to submit an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application with the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: An updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) is mandatory for the Principal Investigator (PI) and all co-Principal Investigators (co-PIs) of a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application.

qa_pairs.question: How can an Alliance Principal Investigator update their Canadian Common CV (CCV) at any time?
qa_pairs.answer: Alliance Principal Investigators can update their Canadian Common CV (CCV) at any time by navigating to 'My Account → View Reporting' on the CCDB.

qa_pairs.question: Who should users contact for technical support related to accessing the Canadian Common CV (CCV) website or for forgotten usernames/passwords?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should contact the CCV helpdesk directly by email at support@cihr-irsc.gc.ca or by phone at 613-954-1968 or 1-888-603-4178 for technical support with the CCV website.

qa_pairs.question: Who should Alliance account holders contact for specific questions about the Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) template or issues with submission to the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: Alliance account holders should contact support@tech.alliancecan.ca for specific questions about the Alliance CCV template or issues submitting their CCV to the Alliance.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process to ensure a submitted Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) is properly linked to an Alliance Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application?
qa_pairs.answer: After successfully submitting an Alliance CCV, users must make note of the confirmation number and enter it on the CCDB to link their RAC application with their CCV submission.

qa_pairs.question: Which sections of the Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) template are considered mandatory?
qa_pairs.answer: The mandatory sections for the Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV) template are Personal Information, Education, Employment, and Research Funding History (over the last five years).

qa_pairs.question: How recent must a Canadian Common CV (CCV) submission be for it to be valid for renewing an Alliance account?
qa_pairs.answer: For Alliance account renewal, a Canadian Common CV (CCV) must have been submitted within the last 300 days.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum age for a Co-Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) for a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application to be considered valid?
qa_pairs.answer: For a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, a Co-Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) must have been submitted within the last 90 days.

qa_pairs.question: When an Academic Principal Investigator submits a new Alliance Canadian Common CV (CCV), how do they report publications enabled by Alliance computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Publications without a provided answer will show with a red border, which the PI must clear by checking the box next to each enabled publication, clicking twice for not enabled publications, or using 'Select all new' or 'Clear all new' buttons.

qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence if an Academic Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file is older than 90 days when applying for an Alliance Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Academic Principal Investigator's Canadian Common CV (CCV) on file is older than 90 days, they will be required to submit a new CCV with their Alliance Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it important for users to report publications enabled by Alliance computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: Reporting publications enabled by Alliance computational resources strengthens the case for additional compute and storage resources and serves as an input for the scientific review process for Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) applications.

qa_pairs.question: Can a Canadian Common CV (CCV) confirmation number from a non-Alliance template, such as NSERC or CIHR, be used for Alliance submissions?
qa_pairs.answer: No, only confirmation numbers for Canadian Common CVs (CCVs) submitted using the Alliance template will be accepted for Alliance submissions; those from other agencies like NSERC or CIHR cannot be used.
